# remnawave_user_action (Resource)

Performs an imperative one-shot action on a Remnawave user. Supported actions:

| Action | Endpoint |
|---|---|
| `enable` | `POST /api/users/actions/:uuid/enable` |
| `disable` | `POST /api/users/actions/:uuid/disable` |
| `reset_traffic` | `POST /api/users/actions/:uuid/reset-traffic` |
| `revoke_subscription` | `POST /api/users/actions/:uuid/revoke-subscription` |

The action is executed on create and re-executed whenever the `triggers` list changes value. This makes the resource suitable for recurring operations such as periodic traffic resets.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "remnawave_user" "test" {
  username            = "my-user"
  expire_at           = "2027-01-01T00:00:00.000Z"
  traffic_limit_bytes = 10737418240
}

# Reset traffic once on creation
resource "remnawave_user_action" "reset" {
  user_uuid = remnawave_user.test.uuid
  action    = "reset_traffic"
  triggers  = [timestamp()]
}
```

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# Import by <user_uuid>:<action>
terraform import remnawave_user_action.reset "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx:reset_traffic"
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `action` (String) Action to perform. One of: enable, disable, reset_traffic, revoke_subscription.
- `user_uuid` (String) UUID of the target user.

### Optional

- `triggers` (List of String) Arbitrary list of strings. When any value changes, the action is re-executed. Commonly set to `[timestamp()]` for periodic re-runs.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Composite identifier: `<user_uuid>:<action>`.
